description: Fix E2E test infrastructure issues: global-teardown orphan kill safety, npm-discovery temp dir leak on server start failure, and browser-tools waitForExecFileCleanup returning timeout instead of success when adapters directory does not exist.

userStories.title: Fix global-teardown pgrep pattern matching unrelated processes
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the global teardown orphan killer to only kill processes from the current test run so that parallel test runs or developer processes are not affected.
acceptanceCriteria: findPidsUnix excludes its own pgrep child process (pgrep -f matches itself)
The pattern is scoped to match only server/test-server process command lines, not arbitrary processes with 'opentabs-e2e-' in an env var path argument
Alternatively, use pgrep's --exact or a more precise matching strategy
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/global-teardown.ts, lines 25-38 (findPidsUnix function).

The current pattern `pgrep -f "opentabs-e2e-"` is overly broad. It matches any process whose command line contains 'opentabs-e2e-' — which includes processes that merely have an OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR env var containing a temp path like '/tmp/opentabs-e2e-config-abc123/'. This could match the test's own Chromium instances, other agents' test processes, etc.

The fix should either:
1. Use a more specific pattern like `pgrep -f 'node.*opentabs-e2e-'` to limit to Node processes, OR
2. Filter the pgrep results by checking if the matched PID is actually a server process (not a browser), OR
3. Use `pgrep -f` with the specific server entry point patterns (e.g., 'dev-proxy.js', 'test-server.ts') combined with the naming convention.

Also note: pgrep -f can match itself (the pgrep process command line contains the pattern). The current filter only excludes `process.pid` (the Node.js process running global-teardown), not the pgrep subprocess. This is usually harmless since pgrep exits before the kill loop, but it's worth adding a comment or using pgrep's own PID exclusion if available.

userStories.title: Fix npm-discovery temp dir leak when startMcpServer fails
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want npm-discovery E2E tests to clean up temp directories even when server startup fails so that repeated test failures don't fill up /tmp.
acceptanceCriteria: Temp directory (npmGlobalDir / fakePrefixDir) is cleaned up in a finally block that runs regardless of whether startMcpServer succeeds
The cleanup order handles the case where server was never assigned (startMcpServer rejected)

userStories.title: Fix waitForExecFileCleanup timeout when adapters directory is missing
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want waitForExecFileCleanup to return success (not timeout) when the adapters directory does not exist, since a missing directory means no leftover exec files.
acceptanceCriteria: When the adapters directory does not exist (ENOENT from readdirSync), the function treats it as 'clean' (no exec files) and returns immediately
The existing behavior for an existing directory with exec files is preserved (continues polling until files are gone or timeout)
